description:
# Why

bl-7523 wrote `delivered_repo` alongside `delivered_in` on every fresh
delivery so a reader knows which code repo owns the squash sha. The
*write* side landed; the *read* side did not. Today:

- `bl show --json` exposes `delivered_in_resolved` and
  `delivered_in_hint_stale` derived from a local scan of the
  integration branch (`delivery::resolve`).
- On a master hub with no code (the bare task hub from bl-ffb4), the
  local scan returns None and `delivered_in_resolved` is null even
  though `delivered_repo` is sitting right there in the task file.
- Same gap on a sibling client: client B reading a task delivered by
  client A sees `delivered_in: <sha>` and `delivered_repo: A` but
  `delivered_in_resolved: null` because the sha isn't on B's main.

So any tooling that walks `delivered_in_resolved` — release-plz's
delivery walks, the github-issues plugin's task-to-commit threading,
sync's half-push detector, dashboards — fragments along repo
boundaries the moment a project goes multi-repo. The provenance is
recorded but not actionable.

The bl-7523 PR's display layer mitigates this for humans: a 'delivered
repo:' line now appears in `bl show` so an operator can manually
navigate. Tooling can't.

# Scope

Resolve `delivered_in` *transparently* through `delivered_repo`:

- If `delivered_in` is reachable on the local integration branch, the
  current path is byte-identical (no extra fetches, no new failure
  modes).
- Otherwise, when `delivered_repo` is set and points at a URL the
  local clone can fetch, fetch it (cache under `.balls/state-repo/`
  or a sibling refs dir — open) and re-run the tag scan against the
  fetched ref.
- `delivered_in_resolved` then exposes the sha; `delivered_in_hint_stale`
  reflects whether the cached sha matched. A new sibling field — name
  TBD, maybe `delivered_in_resolved_repo` — tells the caller which
  repo the resolution came from, so a downstream tool can construct a
  forge URL or `git show` it themselves.

# Open questions

- **Where to cache the fetched refs.** Adding a remote per
  `delivered_repo` to `.balls/state-repo/` works but conflates
  task-store git with code git. A separate `.balls/code-refs/` clone
  is cleaner but doubles git-dir state. Probably reuse state-repo's
  remote machinery — they're both balls-owned and disposable.
- **Auth and rate-limit surface.** Fetching from arbitrary forge URLs
  on every `bl show` is rude. Cache aggressively; gate behind an opt-in
  (`bl show --resolve-remote` or a config flag) for the first cut.
- **Behavior when the URL is unreachable.** Match bl-ffb4's
  safe-but-unlinked stance: warn once, return null, do not break the
  command. A master_url that fails hard-fails *prime*; here the cost
  of failure is just an unresolvable sha, so soft-fail is right.
- **What to do with the existing `delivered_in_hint_stale` semantic.**
  Today 'stale' means 'the cached sha disagrees with the tag scan on
  local main.' With remote resolution, 'stale' could also mean 'the
  cached sha doesn't match the tag in the named repo.' Probably keep
  the existing meaning and add a third field rather than overload.

# Origin

Footgun #2 from the bl-7523 review pass — the *write* side of
provenance landed in e7c03e7 but the *read* side that makes it
useful for tooling is still missing.
title: delivered_in_resolved: transparent cross-repo resolution via delivered_repo
